title: Implement review-driven onboarding, i18n, and quality improvements

sources.reason: Review feedback on onboarding friction, i18n gaps, and stack tier disclosure.
Existing adoption checklist to consolidate into ai-onboard.
Doctor diagnostics reused by onboarding phase 1.
Hosted CI evidence for updating verified stack tiers in documentation.

riskAssessment.reason: Documentation tier updates must stay aligned with system_invariants.json and README markers.

executionDecision.reason: Scope, sources, acceptance, and verification are explicit.

preReviewWarnings: Verify stack tier markers match system_invariants.json after moving hosted CI stacks to verified.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Multi-file governance and documentation change spanning runtime entrypoints.

acceptance: make ai-onboard runs a three-phase guided flow that consolidates doctor, calibrate, and readiness guidance.
.ai/cockpit/README.ja.md and adoption.ja.md provide Japanese runtime documentation aligned with overview.ja.md style.
docs/non-make-adaptation.ja.md documents npm scripts, just, and task bridging patterns.
Hosted CI stacks are recorded as verified in system_invariants.json and README tier markers.
ai_doctor.py and ai_finish.py meet raised critical coverage floors with additional tests.
make quality passes including check-docs-metadata and check-ai-system-invariants.
guidelines: New public Make targets must appear in Makefile.ai template and help output.
Japanese cockpit docs must use front matter with author: Ray and avoid banned terminology from JAPANESE_STYLE_RULES.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User requested review-driven improvements to cockpit runtime docs, system invariants, and Make entrypoints.

rollbackNote: Revert onboarding script, Japanese cockpit docs, tier marker updates, and coverage floor changes.
